dincerGucyeter.title: Public Reading Evening and Discussion with German Writer Dinçer Güçyeter
dincerGucyeter.location: Cinema Arta | Cluj-Napoca,  Str. Universității nr. 3
dincerGucyeter.description: On 6 November 2025, an evening of reading and dialogue took place with German writer Dinçer Güçyeter, dedicated to his award-winning novel "O poveste despre Germania noastră" – "Unser Deutschlandmärchen", alongside translator Manuela Klenke, director Lina Vdovîi, and sociologist Sorin Gog.

celaMasterclass.title: CELA Masterclass with Manuela Klenke (Germany) and Corina Oproae (Spain)
celaMasterclass.description: CELA masterclass with Manuela Klenke (Germany) and Corina Oproae (Spain): Migration and double identity – translators and writers as "double-agents".

dieGuteTage.title: Die guten Tage VI
dieGuteTage.location: Literarisches Colloquium Berlin, Am Sandwerder 5, 14109 Berlin
dieGuteTage.description: An evening celebrating Southeast European poetry, jointly organised by TRADUKI and the Literarisches Colloquium Berlin. Manuela Klenke translated and read from "re.volver" by Romanian poet Livia Ștefan — a powerful collection exploring trauma, memory, and personal transformation.

leipzigerBuchmesse.title: Worte bewegen Welten – Leipziger Buchmesse 2025
leipzigerBuchmesse.description: One of Europe's most important spring events for the book and media industry, held in Leipzig from March 27–30, 2025. The fair brought together readers, authors, publishers, and media from around the world. Manuela Klenke and Alexandru Bulucz presented their translations from Romanian: Re.volver by Livia Ştefan (parasitenpresse) and Metallische Igel by Moni Stănilă (Edition Fototapeta).

viceVersa2023.title: ViceVersa 2023: German-Romanian Translation Workshop
viceVersa2023.description: Together with Jan Schönherr, Manuela Klenke co-led the second German-Romanian ViceVersa Translation Workshop, organised in cooperation with Traduki, the Goethe-Institut Bucharest, the Toledo Programme, and the German Cultural Centre Cluj-Napoca. The workshop brought together translators from both countries to collaborate on literary texts, exploring the nuances of translating between German and Romanian.

salutareBarbarilor.title: German Poetry Anthology: "Salutare, barbarilor!"
salutareBarbarilor.location: Hidden – The Social Space, Strada Doamna Chiajna 26, 031234 București
salutareBarbarilor.description: Joachim Umlauf and Manuela Klenke launch, together with Claudiu Komartin at the Blecher Institute, the German poetry anthology "Salutare, barbarilor!"

naturalezasLiterarias.title: Naturalezas Literarias
naturalezasLiterarias.description: On the occasion of St. George's Day, students of Romance Studies from Osnabrück organised, in collaboration with the Literaturbüro Westniedersachsen and the DE-IT and DE-FR societies, a literary event called „Naturalezas literarias – Romanische Sprachen der Natur", at which Manuela Klenke, together with jazz musician Shabnam Parvaresh, presented a lyrical-musical performance as the Romanian contribution, based on poems by Simona Popescu.
